frameworks.name: pupil-linked arousal
frameworks.one_line: pupil dilation indexes physiological arousal predicting memory encoding

frameworks.name: central arousal systems gating synaptic plasticity
frameworks.one_line: arousal modulates neocortical plasticity supporting rapid memory formation

core_question: Does trial-by-trial pupil dilation during encoding predict which stimuli are later remembered?
key_assumptions: pupil response is a reliable proxy for physiological arousal
arousal at encoding influences long-term memory formation
subsequent-memory effects can be read out trial-by-trial
key_findings: pupil dilation during encoding predicted later recall across modalities
prediction held at immediate test and after 24 hr
effect was strongest for emotionally arousing stimuli
interpretations: the eyes provide a window onto long-term memory formation
central arousal systems support rapid memory, possibly by gating neocortical synaptic plasticity
